scheme_name: Kunwar Bai Nu Mameru Yojana- SEBC & EBC
page_title: Kunwar Bai Nu Mameru Yojana- SEBC & EBC

details: The "Kunwar Bai Nu Mameru Yojana- SEBC & EBC" scheme was launched by the Social Justice and Empowerment Department, Government of Gujarat. The scheme aims to provide financial assistance to families from Socially and Educationally Backward Classes and Economically Backward Classes to help cover the wedding expenses of their daughters. Under this scheme, an amount of ₹12,000 is provided for each daughter’s marriage, subject to a maximum of two adult daughters per family.
benefits: ₹12,000 is provided for the marriage of a daughter.
If the marriage occurred before April 1, 2021, assistance of ₹10,000 is provided.
eligibility: The applicant should be a native of Gujarat.
The applicant must be a woman belonging to a Socially and Educationally Backward Class (SEBC) or Economically Backward Class (EBC).
The annual family income should not exceed ₹6,00,000/-.
The marriage must be legally registered.
The assistance is limited to two daughters per family.

online: The eligible applicant may visit the e-Samaj Kalyan Portal:
On the home page, under the tab ‘Citizen Login’, click on New User ‘
Enter your Full Name as per Aadhaar Card, Gender, Date of Birth, Aadhaar Card Number, Email ID, Caste etc. and then click on ‘Register’.
After successful registration, the applicants can login through their User ID and Password.
Now, click on ‘User Profile’ to update your profile.
Fill in all the mandatory information and then click on ‘Update’.
After updating the profile successfully, select the scheme appearing on the home page; an application form will open.
Fill in all the mandatory details in the application form and upload all the relevant documents.
Now agree with the terms & conditions and click on ‘Save Application’.

Submit the application form and take out the print of the application for future reference. The applicant can note down the application number to track the application status by logging into the portal using their User ID and Password.

documents_required: Annual Income Certificate of the Bride's Father/Guardian
Bank passbook/Cancelled cheque (in the bride's name)

faqs.question: How much assistance is provided under this scheme?
faqs.answer: ₹12,000 per eligible daughter for marriages after April 1, 2021; ₹10,000 for earlier marriages.

faqs.question: How many daughters can get the benefit in one family?
faqs.answer: A maximum of two adult daughters per family can avail the benefit.

faqs.question: Can I apply offline?
faqs.answer: No, only online applications are accepted.

faqs.question: Is there any application fee?
faqs.answer: No, the application is free of cost.

faqs.question: What documents are mandatory for application?
faqs.answer: Aadhaar Card, Income Certificate, Marriage Certificate, Bank Passbook, and Passport Photos.

faqs.question: Can I apply for assistance for more than two daughters?
faqs.answer: No, the benefit is restricted to two daughters per family.

faqs.question: Is the scheme applicable across Gujarat?
faqs.answer: Yes, it is applicable throughout the state.

faqs.question: Can widows or divorced women apply under this scheme?
faqs.answer: No, this scheme is specifically for first-time marriages of eligible daughters.

faqs.question: Where can I check the application status?
faqs.answer: You can log in at https://esamajkalyan.gujarat.gov.in to track your application status.
